text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST — SURVEILLANCE IMAGING

CLINICAL INDICATION:
History of left breast invasive ductal carcinoma, triple-negative, Stage IIB (T2N1M0), treated with neoadjuvant pembrolizumab plus dose-dense AC followed by weekly paclitaxel/pembrolizumab. Underwent left modified radical mastectomy with axillary lymph node dissection and right prophylactic mastectomy, pathology demonstrating residual disease (ypT1cN1a, RCB Class III). Subsequently received adjuvant capecitabine, post-mastectomy radiation therapy, and pembrolizumab (discontinued early for grade 3 immune-related hepatitis on cycle 14). Currently on corticosteroid taper for hepatitis. Known germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant. Presenting for 6-month post-surgical surveillance. Also evaluate for hepatic changes in context of recently elevated liver transaminases related to immune-related hepatitis, now improving on steroids.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis performed with intravenous contrast (iohexol 350 mg I/mL, 120 mL, injected at 3 mL/sec via right antecubital vein). Images obtained in the portal venous phase through the abdomen and pelvis; arterial and delayed phases not obtained. Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformations generated. No oral contrast administered. No known contrast allergies; patient premedicated per protocol given history of prior mild reaction.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Left Chest Wall and Reconstruction:
Post-mastectomy changes are noted on the left, with the expected absence of breast parenchyma. A tissue expander is present in the left anterior chest wall soft tissues, maintaining appropriate position without evidence of malposition, periprosthetic fluid collection, or adjacent soft tissue mass. Skin flap is intact without thickening or nodularity to suggest early skin recurrence. The overlying integument shows mild diffuse thickening and subcutaneous stranding throughout the left anterior chest wall, consistent with the expected sequelae of completed radiation therapy. These findings are stable and post-treatment in character. No discrete enhancing chest wall nodule or soft tissue mass identified to suggest locoregional recurrence along the mastectomy scar or chest wall.

Right Chest Wall:
Tissue expander present in the right anterior chest wall in appropriate position. No periprosthetic collection or adjacent soft tissue abnormality. Normal-appearing post-prophylactic mastectomy changes on the right.

Lungs and Airways:
The lungs are clear without parenchymal consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or suspicious pulmonary nodule. No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax. Airways patent to the segmental level. The trachea is midline.

Mediastinum:
Heart is normal in size without pericardial effusion. Aorta is normal in caliber. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy by size or morphology criteria. No anterior mediastinal or prevascular nodal enlargement. Esophagus is normal in appearance.

Hila:
No hilar lymph node enlargement. No hilar mass.

Axillae:
Left axilla: Post-surgical changes consistent with prior levels I-II axillary lymph node dissection. Fibrofatty stranding in the left axillary bed without discrete residual or recurrent nodal tissue or soft tissue mass. No new left axillary nodularity suspicious for nodal recurrence.
Right axilla: No enlarged or morphologically suspicious lymph nodes.

Supraclavicular Fossae:
No supraclavicular lymph node enlargement bilaterally. No soft tissue mass.

Internal Mammary Region (Left):
The left internal mammary chain shows expected post-radiation changes without discrete nodal enlargement or soft tissue mass suspicious for recurrent disease along the irradiated nodal bed.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size with smooth contours. Hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal hypodense or hypervascular lesion to suggest hepatic metastasis or hepatocellular abnormality. No biliary ductal dilatation. Gallbladder is present and unremarkable. Portal vein is patent with normal hepatopetal flow on enhancement characteristics. No periportal edema or periportal lymphadenopathy. Hepatic morphology is reassuring, without imaging findings to suggest significant ongoing hepatocellular injury or toxicity, consistent with the reported clinical improvement in transaminases.

Spleen:
Normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. No splenic lesion.

Pancreas:
Pancreatic body, head, neck, and tail are normal in attenuation and morphology without mass, ductal dilatation, or peripancreatic inflammatory change.

Kidneys and Ureters:
Bilateral kidneys are normal in size, contour, and parenchymal enhancement. No hydronephrosis, renal mass, or perirenal abnormality. No hydroureter.

Adrenal Glands:
Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in morphology. No adrenal nodule or mass.

Stomach and Bowel:
Stomach, small bowel, and visualized large bowel are without obstruction, wall thickening, or mass lesion. No free air or free fluid.

Lymph Nodes — Abdomen:
No retroperitoneal, para-aortic, mesenteric, or celiac lymph node enlargement. No suspicious intra-abdominal nodal mass.

Vasculature:
Aorta and major abdominal vessels are normal in caliber without aneurysm or significant atherosclerotic disease.

Peritoneum:
No ascites. No peritoneal or omental nodularity or thickening to suggest peritoneal carcinomatosis.

---

PELVIS:

Uterus and Ovaries:
Uterus is normal in size and contour. Ovaries are not discretely visualized on this examination, which is not unusual for this patient's age and body habitus. No adnexal mass or pelvic free fluid.

Lymph Nodes — Pelvis:
No external or internal iliac, obturator, or inguinal lymph node enlargement.

Bladder:
Urinary bladder is partially distended and unremarkable without wall thickening or intraluminal mass.

Bony Pelvis and Visualized Skeleton:
No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion in the visualized axial or appendicular skeleton to suggest osseous metastatic disease. Pelvic bony structures are intact. Vertebral bodies and posterior elements of the thoracic and lumbar spine show no suspicious marrow replacement or cortical destruction. No pathologic fracture.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No imaging evidence of locoregional recurrence in the left chest wall, bilateral axillae, supraclavicular fossae, or internal mammary chain.

2. Post-mastectomy changes bilaterally with tissue expanders in appropriate positions. Left chest wall soft tissue changes consistent with prior radiation therapy, stable and post-treatment in appearance.

3. No hepatic lesion, hepatic mass, or imaging finding to suggest ongoing hepatotoxicity or metastatic involvement. Hepatic parenchyma appears normal, consistent with biochemical improvement in transaminases reported clinically.

4. No pulmonary nodule, pleural effusion, mediastinal adenopathy, or other thoracic finding suggestive of metastatic disease.

5. No abdominal or pelvic lymphadenopathy, peritoneal disease, adrenal lesion, or suspicious osseous finding.

6. Overall, this examination is reassuring for absence of distant or locoregional metastatic disease at approximately 6 months following surgical resection.

Reviewing Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist Signature]
Dictated and finalized electronically.
